coredata.dc:title: The isothermal section of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2005 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.
coredata.dc:creator: Jingqi, Liu
Jiqin, Qian
Ren, Ding
Yinghong, Zhuang
Jinli, Huang
coredata.dc:description: The isothermal section of the phase diagram of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K has been investigated mainly by X-ray powder diffraction with the aid of differential thermal analysis. The 773K isothermal section of the ternary system consists of 11 single-phase regions, 19 two-phase regions and 9 three-phase regions. At 773K, the maximum solid solubility of Ti in Mn, GdMn12, Gd6Mn23 and GdMn2 are about 8, 2, 3 and 3at% Ti, respectively. The homogeneity range of Mn2Ti extends from about 61 to 69at% Mn. No ternary compounds were observed in this system.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Phase diagram
Isothermal section
Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The isothermal section of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The isothermal section of the Gd–Mn–Ti system at 773K.
Crystal structure data of the phases existing in the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K
The phases composition in the three-phase regions

article.ce:title: The isothermal section of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Nonferrous Metal Materials and New Processing Technology, Guangxi University, Ministry of Education, Nanning, Guangxi 530004, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The isothermal section of the phase diagram of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K has been investigated mainly by X-ray powder diffraction with the aid of differential thermal analysis. The 773K isothermal section of the ternary system consists of 11 single-phase regions, 19 two-phase regions and 9 three-phase regions. At 773K, the maximum solid solubility of Ti in Mn, GdMn12, Gd6Mn23 and GdMn2 are about 8, 2, 3 and 3at% Ti, respectively. The homogeneity range of Mn2Ti extends from about 61 to 69at% Mn. No ternary compounds were observed in this system.

ce:section.ce:para: Alloys based on Ti such as Ti–Mn and Ti–Cr are accepted as high capacity electrode materials. While developing these kinds of alloy, it is very important to retain the Laves phase structure. The intermetallic compound based on Mn2Ti has wider homogeneity range than TiCr2. So many authors developed intermetallic compound based on Mn2Ti. There are indications that the electrode performance may be improved when Mn or Ti in Mn2Ti alloys is replaced by other elements (e.g. Zr) and the atomic radius of the other elements is larger than the atomic radius of Mn or Ti. Under this condition, the unit cell of the Mn2Ti phase expands and the stability of the stored hydrogen in Mn2Ti is improved [1]. The atomic radius of Mn, Ti, Zr and Gd are 136.6, 144.8, 159.7 and 180.1pm, respectively. If Mn or Ti in Mn2Ti alloy is replaced by Gd, the electrode performance of the Mn2Ti might be improved. When studying phase diagrams one may find new intermetallic compounds. The performance of materials not only depends on the properties of the single-phase in the materials, but it is also influenced by mutual interactions of the phases in the materials. The phase relationships in the materials are defined by the phase diagram. Consequently, the study, the thermodynamics and the phase diagrams of alloys is an effective way for finding new materials and improve the original materials.
The Gd–Ti binary system has been investigated and determined, no intermetallic phase was found in this system [2].
Refs. [3,4] reported on the Gd–Mn binary phase diagram. Three intermetallic compounds GdMn2, Gd6Mn23 and GdMn12 were found.
Refs. [5,6] reported on the Mn–Ti binary phase diagram. There are three intermetallic compounds in the binary system at 773K, namely αMnTi, βMnTi and Mn2Ti.
The Gd–Mn–Ni and Gd–Ni–Ti ternary phase diagrams have been published previously [7,8]. Up to the present, the phase diagram of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system has not yet been reported.

The samples (each weighing 2.5g) were prepared by high frequency induction melting in a sintered Al2O3 crucible under high purity argon. The purity of Gd, Mn and Ti used in this work are 99.9, 99.8 and 99.99%, respectively. During melting we used an as low as possible electric current to minimize the loss of Mn by vaporizing. An extra amount of Mn (2wt%) was added before melting to compensate the loss. All samples after melting were sealed in evacuated quartz tubes for a homogenization heat treatment. The homogenization temperatures were chosen on the basis of the binary phase diagrams of the Gd–Mn, Mn–Ti and Gd–Ti systems. The samples, with less than 50.5% Mn, were annealed at 1053K for 15 days, then they were cooled at a rate of 9K/h to 823K and kept at this temperature for 10 days. The samples which more than 50.5% Mn, were annealed at 1053K for 15 days. At last all the samples were cooled at a rate of 5K/h to 773K for 15 days. Then they were quenched into liquid nitrogen.

X-ray powder diffraction and metallography were used to analyze the samples. The homogenized samples were broken into two parts; one of the two parts was used for X-ray powder diffraction and the other for metallographic investigation. The samples for X-ray diffraction analysis were ground into powder, annealed at 773K for 4 days in vacuum glass tubes and quenched into liquid nitrogen. The X-ray powder diffraction was carried out on a Rigaku D/max 2500V diffractometer with Cu Kα radiation and graphite monochromator operated at 40KV and 200mA. The Materials Data Inc. software Jade 5.0 [9] and a Powder Diffraction File (PDF release 2002) were used to determine the phase relationship.
In the Gd–Ti system, we found no binary compounds and confirmed that Gd and Ti have no mutual solubility at 773K. The result agrees with ref. [2].
Three binary phases GdMn2, Gd6Mn23 and GdMn12 have been confirmed in the Gd–Mn system at 773K. Our result obtained agrees with refs. [3,4].

There exist different results for the intermetallic compounds in the Mn–Ti binary system. Refs. [5,6] concluded that there exist three intermetallic compounds, αMnTi, βMnTi and Mn2Ti in this system at 773K. Refs. [5,6] reported that the region between 50 and 100at% Ti does not contain intermetallic compounds at 773K and that there exist a phase transition at 823K. No endothermal or exothermal peak has been observed in the DTA curves obtained in our work in this region at 823K. We did observe the intermetallic compound Mn15Ti85. Ref. [10] reported that Mn15Ti85 and Mn5Ti exist in this system. The intermetallic compounds αMnTi, βMnTi, Mn2Ti, Mn15Ti85 and Mn5Ti have been observed in our work at 773K. Other intermetallic compounds have not been observed at 773K.

In order to determine the solid solubility exactly, we prepared 60 samples in the high frequency induction furnace. The results show that the maximum solid solubility of Ti in Mn, GdMn12, Gd6Mn23 and GdMn2 is about 8, 2, 3 and 3at% Ti, respectively at 773K. The Mn2Ti compound was observed to have a homogeneity range. The homogeneity range of Mn2Ti extends from about 61 to 69at% Mn at 773K.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The isothermal section of the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K
ce:section.ce:para: By comparing and analyzing the X-ray diffraction patterns of 225 samples and by identifying the phases in each sample, the isothermal section of the phase diagram of the ternary system Gd–Mn–Ti has been determined at 773K. Fig. 1 shows the isothermal section. It consists of 11 single-phase regions, 19 two-phase regions and 9 three-phase regions. The 11 single-phase regions are: A (Gd), B (Mn), C (Ti), D (GdMn2), E (Gd6Mn23), F (GdMn12), G (Mn5Ti), H (Mn2Ti), I (βMnTi), J (αMnTi) and K (Mn15Ti85). The 19 two-phase regions are: A+C, A+D, A+H, A+I, A+J, A+K, D+H, D+E, E+F, E+B, E+G, E+H, F+B, B+G, G+H, H+I, I+J, J+K and K+C. The 9 three-phase regions are: A+D+H, A+H+I, A+I+J, A+J+K, A+K+C, D+E+H, E+F+B, E+B+G and E+G+H. Crystal structure data of the phases existing in the Gd–Mn–Ti ternary system at 773K are listed in Table 1 . The phases composition in the three-phase regions are listed in Table 2 . No ternary compounds were observed in this isothermal section.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The work was jointly supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China and by the Natural Science Foundation of Guangxi (Gui Ke 0447037).

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Phase Diagrams of Binary Titanium Alloys

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: Materials Data Relcase5, XRD Processing, Materials Data Inc. (MDI).

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Pearson's Handbook, Desk Edition, Crystallographic Data for Intermetallic Phases

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: Powder Diffraction File, International Center for Diffraction Data, Pennsylvania, 1993.
